tagline: Same instincts. Ten times the intel.

round_of_64.reasoning: AdjEM gap of 39.0 is the largest in the East region. Duke's AdjO of 128.1 ranks 1st nationally and their Barthag of 0.981 is near-perfect. Historical twins include 2024 Purdue (Runner-Up) and 2025 Florida (Champion) — elite company. No pathway for a 16-seed with AdjEM of -1.7.

Ohio State's AdjEM of 23.6 significantly exceeds TCU's 15.2, an 8.4-point gap that places this outside the typical 8-9 coin-flip range. Ohio State's AdjO of 125.2 is elite (top-15 nationally) and their Barthag of 0.917 dwarfs TCU's 0.836. TCU's archetype twins went deep (2021 UCLA to F4) but the efficiency gap here is too large for the 9-seed.

14.6 AdjEM gap puts this well outside upset territory. St. John's AdjO of 119.8 and AdjD of 94.1 create a balanced elite profile. Northern Iowa's archetypes (2011 Butler, 2010 Butler) went deep as mid-majors, but those Butler teams had AdjEM profiles 5+ points higher relative to their fields. St. John's tempo advantage (69.6 vs 62.2) will force Northern Iowa out of their comfort zone.

16.3 AdjEM gap and Kansas's WAB of 6.3 vs Cal Baptist's -2.3 make this decisive. Kansas's archetype twins (2018 Michigan Runner-Up, 2010 Duke Champion) confirm this is an elite-caliber team. Cal Baptist's twin 2022 Saint Peter's went to E8, but Saint Peter's had a uniquely favorable bracket path — Cal Baptist faces a 4-seed, not a weakened 2.

10.5 AdjEM gap favors Louisville but South Florida's archetypes (2022 Iowa State, 2024 Colorado — both Sweet 16) suggest a team capable of a run. Louisville's AdjO of 124.0 and top archetype twin (2016 Villanova Champion) are compelling. The gap is just large enough at 10.5 to take the favorite, but South Florida's tempo (71.4) and offensive efficiency could keep this close.

21.3 AdjEM gap is decisive. Michigan State's Barthag of 0.944 and WAB of 7.4 are among the best in the field. NDSU's AdjD of 106.0 will be exposed by MSU's 122.9 AdjO. Izzo's deep tournament pedigree adds an unquantifiable but historically measurable coaching edge in R64.

8.4 AdjEM gap in UCLA's favor with UCLA's AdjO of 124.5 vs UCF's 120.0. Both teams' archetypes went deep (UCLA twins: 2013 Syracuse F4, 2018 Michigan Runner-Up; UCF twins: multiple Sweet 16s). UCLA's WAB of 3.6 vs UCF's 2.1 and significantly better AdjD (101.8 vs 105.7) give them the edge, but this is a competitive 7-10 matchup.

30.2 AdjEM gap is insurmountable. UConn's AdjO of 127.3 is 2nd nationally and their Barthag of 0.961 ranks in the top 5. Furman's AdjEM of -2.9 puts them among the weakest 15-seeds in the field. Historical 2-15 upset rate requires a gap under 12.0 — this gap is nearly triple that threshold.

43.8 AdjEM gap is the largest in the entire tournament. Florida's archetype twin is 2025 Florida (Champion, 0.953 similarity) — essentially the same team returning. Prairie View A&M's AdjEM of -10.3 makes this the most lopsided matchup in the bracket. No statistical basis for any other pick.

Iowa's AdjEM of 21.9 actually exceeds Clemson's 19.6 despite being the 9-seed. Iowa's AdjO of 122.1 significantly outpaces Clemson's 116.5, and Iowa's Barthag of 0.907 edges Clemson's 0.893. Both teams have strong archetype twins (both map to 2013 Syracuse F4). Iowa's offensive firepower is the tiebreaker in this coin-flip matchup.

17.1 AdjEM gap puts this comfortably outside upset territory. Vanderbilt's AdjD of 94.7 will contain McNeese's offense. McNeese's archetype twin (2016 Syracuse F4) suggests a team that could overperform, but that Syracuse team had an AdjEM 5 points higher relative to their field. Vanderbilt's WAB of 5.5 vs McNeese's -1.4 confirms the quality gap.

22.1 AdjEM gap is decisive. Nebraska's Barthag of 0.929 ranks in the top 20 nationally. Troy's AdjEM of 5.2 and WAB of -4.6 indicate they were barely a tournament-level team. Nebraska's archetype twin (2012 Syracuse E8) confirms this is a team built for tournament success. 4-13 upset rate requires a much smaller efficiency gap.

5.2 AdjEM gap is narrow for a 6-11 matchup but UNC's WAB of 4.9 vs VCU's 1.7 reveals stronger quality wins. UNC's AdjO of 121.5 and archetype twins (2018 Michigan Runner-Up, 2014 UConn Champion) suggest a team with deep-run potential. VCU's archetypes are consistently Sweet 16 exits — good but capped. UNC's overall talent profile and brand of efficiency narrowly carry this.

32.6 AdjEM gap is among the largest in the tournament. Illinois's AdjEM of 28.9 ranks 5th nationally and their Barthag of 0.966 is elite. Penn's AdjEM of -3.8 after SOS adjustment makes this functionally a bye game. Illinois archetype twin 2024 Purdue (Runner-Up) confirms championship-caliber quality.

Despite Saint Mary's 3.3 AdjEM edge, Texas A&M's AdjO of 119.9 matches Saint Mary's 119.4 and TAMU played in the SEC (SOS 0.728 vs SMC's 0.387). A&M's archetype twin 2018 Kentucky (E8) shows tournament resilience in high-major talent. Saint Mary's WCC schedule inflates their efficiency metrics — SOS-adjusted, this gap narrows to near zero. Texas A&M's conference-calibrated quality gives them the slight edge.

30.3 AdjEM gap driven by Houston's AdjD of 89.5, the 2nd-best defense in the country. Idaho's AdjEM of -4.5 with no path to generating enough scoring against this defense. Houston's archetype twin (2015 Wisconsin Runner-Up) confirms deep-run caliber. Upset probability is near zero.

41.6 AdjEM gap is the 2nd-largest in the tournament. Arizona's AdjO of 128.7 and AdjEM of 33.6 rank 2nd and 3rd nationally. Archetype twin 2025 Florida (Champion) signals the highest ceiling in the field. LIU's AdjEM of -8.0 offers zero competitive pathway.

Utah State's AdjEM of 20.9 actually exceeds Villanova's 19.1 despite being the 9-seed. Utah State's AdjO of 123.0 edges Villanova's 119.6, and their Barthag of 0.895 tops Villanova's 0.881. Both teams map to strong archetype twins (both link to 2021 UCLA F4). In this true toss-up, the efficiency edge belongs to Utah State.

17.4 AdjEM gap is comfortable. Wisconsin's Barthag of 0.918 and WAB of 4.5 confirm they are a legitimate top-25 team. High Point's AdjEM of 7.7 and WAB of -3.3 place them among the weaker 12-seeds. High Point's archetype twin (2012 Ohio S16) is interesting but Ohio was a 13-seed that benefited from a historically weak bracket pod.

21.2 AdjEM gap is decisive. Arkansas's Barthag of 0.930 and WAB of 5.3 confirm elite quality. Hawaii's AdjEM of 6.5 and weak SOS of 0.328 suggest their metrics are inflated by a weak schedule. The 4-13 upset rate requires a much smaller gap than 21.2.

2.8 AdjEM gap is narrow but BYU's WAB of 4.5 vs Texas's -0.2 reveals a massive quality-wins disparity. Texas played in the SEC but underperformed expectations (negative WAB despite high-major schedule). BYU's archetype twin (2016 Oklahoma F4) signals a team with tournament upside. Texas's metrics are inflated by schedule-adjusted regression.

25.0 AdjEM gap is comfortable. Gonzaga's AdjEM of 25.8 and archetype twin (2025 Tennessee E8, 0.959 similarity) confirm deep-run caliber. Kennesaw State's AdjEM of 0.8 makes them one of the weakest 14-seeds. Gonzaga's offensive efficiency of 123.7 will overwhelm Kennesaw's defense.

2.7 AdjEM gap is a coin flip by the numbers, but Miami's WAB of 3.8 vs Missouri's 1.2 indicates Miami has been better against quality opponents. Miami's archetype twins (2021 UCLA F4, 2014 UConn Champion) show higher tournament ceilings than Missouri's Sweet 16 archetypes. Miami's AdjD of 101.5 edges Missouri's 103.0 in what should be a half-court battle.

35.5 AdjEM gap is overwhelming. Purdue's AdjEM of 27.6 with a Barthag of 0.951 ranks in the top 8 nationally. Queens' AdjEM of -7.9 offers no competitive basis. Purdue's archetype twin (2024 Purdue Runner-Up) is literally themselves from two years ago. This is a formality.

38.3 AdjEM gap is the 4th-largest in the tournament. Michigan's AdjEM of 30.0 and Barthag of 0.967 rank in the top 5 nationally. Archetype twin 2012 Kentucky (Champion, 0.943 similarity) is the strongest possible signal. UMBC's AdjEM of -8.3 — even their famous 2018 upset came against a Virginia team that ranked 1st but had a 16-seed with AdjEM of +5, not -8.

2.2 AdjEM gap is essentially a coin flip. Georgia's AdjO of 124.3 significantly outpaces Saint Louis's 119.6, and Georgia's WAB of 2.6 edges SLU's 1.9. Georgia's archetype twins (2016 Oklahoma F4, 2024 Alabama F4) suggest higher tournament upside than Saint Louis's Sweet 16 archetypes. Georgia's SEC pedigree and offensive firepower are marginal tiebreakers.

15.7 AdjEM gap is solid. Texas Tech's AdjD of 93.6 is elite (top 20 nationally) and will stifle Akron's offense. Texas Tech's archetype twin (2024 Purdue Runner-Up) confirms top-tier caliber. Akron's archetype twin (2014 Stanford S16) went deep but Stanford had significantly better defensive metrics than Akron's 106.5 AdjD.

18.5 AdjEM gap is comfortable. Alabama's AdjEM of 25.7 and Barthag of 0.935 rank in the top 15. Hofstra's AdjEM of 7.2 and weak SOS of 0.378 inflate their metrics from a weak conference. Alabama's archetype twin (2016 Oklahoma F4) suggests a team with explosive offensive potential. Hofstra's archetype twin (2010 Cornell S16) is intriguing but Cornell faced a uniquely weak bracket pod.

7.9 AdjEM gap is meaningful but not insurmountable. Tennessee's AdjD of 95.5 is elite and will challenge SMU's AdjO of 123.0. Tennessee's Barthag of 0.941 vs SMU's 0.861 reveals a significant quality gap beyond raw AdjEM. Tennessee's archetype twins (2012 Syracuse E8, 2024 Purdue Runner-Up) signal deep-run potential. SMU's negative WAB (-0.0) suggests they underperformed their talent level.

23.0 AdjEM gap is decisive. Virginia's AdjEM of 26.2 and Barthag of 0.942 rank in the top 12 nationally. Wright State's AdjEM of 3.3 and WAB of -3.2 confirm a below-average tournament team. Virginia's archetype twin (2024 Purdue Runner-Up) signals deep-run caliber. Wright State's archetypes are limited to R64/R32 exits.

Santa Clara's AdjEM of 21.3 actually exceeds Kentucky's 19.9 despite being the 10-seed. Santa Clara's AdjO of 125.0 tops Kentucky's 119.9 by a significant margin, and their Barthag of 0.895 edges Kentucky's 0.890. Santa Clara's archetype twin (2016 Oklahoma F4) has deep-run pedigree. The data says Santa Clara is genuinely the better team — picking them is following the numbers, not chasing an upset.

35.3 AdjEM gap is overwhelming. Iowa State's AdjEM of 28.2 with Barthag of 0.958 ranks 6th nationally. Archetype twin 2024 Purdue (Runner-Up, 0.944 similarity) confirms championship-caliber efficiency. Tennessee State's AdjEM of -7.1 provides no competitive basis whatsoever.

round_of_32.reasoning: 13.7 AdjEM gap is decisive in R32. Duke's AdjO of 128.1 is the best in the country and will overwhelm Ohio State's AdjD of 101.6. Duke's archetype twins (2024 Purdue Runner-Up, 2025 Florida Champion) confirm championship-caliber quality. Ohio State's strong archetypes (2013 Syracuse F4) don't overcome the raw efficiency gap.

St. John's holds a 2.2 AdjEM edge with superior Barthag (0.941 vs 0.928) and WAB (7.3 vs 6.3). SJU's archetype twin 2023 Connecticut (Champion, 0.97 similarity) is the strongest signal in this matchup. Kansas's archetypes are also strong (2010 Duke Champion) but the efficiency edge belongs to St. John's. A true coin-flip elevated by SJU's marginally better data profile.

Michigan State edges Louisville by 0.9 AdjEM but the decisive factor is WAB: 7.4 vs 4.0. MSU has proven significantly more against quality opponents, suggesting Louisville's efficiency is somewhat schedule-inflated. MSU's Barthag of 0.944 edges Louisville's 0.936. Izzo's tournament pedigree provides a marginal coaching edge in a game this close.

5.4 AdjEM gap in UConn's favor with their AdjO of 127.3 creating matchup problems UCLA's AdjD of 101.8 cannot solve. UConn's Barthag of 0.961 dwarfs UCLA's 0.911 — a quality gap larger than the AdjEM alone suggests. UCLA's archetypes went deep but UConn's raw efficiency is a tier above.

11.9 AdjEM gap favors Florida substantially. Florida's archetype twin is literally 2025 Florida (Champion) at 0.953 similarity — this team knows how to win in March. Iowa's offensive firepower (AdjO 122.1) could keep it interesting but Florida's AdjD of 91.8 is elite enough to contain Iowa's scoring. Florida's Barthag of 0.970 is among the highest in the field.

4.3 AdjEM gap in Vanderbilt's favor with significantly higher Barthag (0.946 vs 0.931). Vanderbilt's archetype twins (2016 Oklahoma F4, 2016 Villanova Champion) suggest deep-run potential. Nebraska's archetypes are also strong (2010 Duke Champion) but Vanderbilt's efficiency profile is clearly superior. WAB is nearly tied (7.3 vs 7.5), confirming both teams beat quality opponents — but Vandy does it more efficiently.

12.3 AdjEM gap is substantial. Illinois's AdjEM of 28.9 ranks 5th nationally while UNC sits at 21.4. Illinois's Barthag of 0.966 vs UNC's 0.903 reveals a significant quality tier difference. UNC's brand name and tournament experience don't overcome a gap this large. Illinois archetype twin 2024 Purdue (Runner-Up) confirms they belong in the conversation.

14.5 AdjEM gap driven by Houston's AdjD of 89.5, the 2nd-best defense in the country. Texas A&M's AdjO of 119.9 will be severely tested against this defense. Houston's WAB of 8.2 dwarfs TAMU's 1.6, confirming dominance against quality opponents. TAMU got here as a 10-seed upset — their run ends against a genuine elite team.

14.6 AdjEM gap is decisive. Arizona's AdjO of 128.7 and AdjEM of 33.6 rank among the top 3 nationally. Utah State's AdjEM of 20.9 is respectable for a 9-seed but the gap is too wide. Arizona's archetype twin 2025 Florida (Champion) signals the highest ceiling in the West region.

0.7 AdjEM gap is essentially nothing, but Arkansas's WAB of 7.5 vs Wisconsin's 5.4 reveals meaningfully better quality wins. Arkansas's Barthag of 0.934 marginally edges Wisconsin's 0.929. Both have deep-run archetypes (2016 Oklahoma F4) but Arkansas's resume is slightly stronger. A true toss-up tilted by quality-of-wins analysis.

5.8 AdjEM gap in Gonzaga's favor with archetype twin 2025 Tennessee (E8, 0.959 similarity) confirming tournament-caliber quality. BYU's AdjO of 124.7 is dangerous but Gonzaga's defense and overall efficiency profile are a clear tier above. Gonzaga has deep tournament pedigree at the coaching level that matters in R32.

13.4 AdjEM gap is decisive. Purdue's AdjEM of 27.6 with Barthag of 0.951 ranks in the top 8 nationally. Miami's AdjEM of 19.6 is respectable but the gap is too large. Purdue's archetype twin 2024 Purdue (Runner-Up) is literally themselves — known tournament performers. Miami's archetypes (2021 UCLA F4) don't close this efficiency gap.

17.0 AdjEM gap is the largest remaining in R32. Michigan's AdjEM of 30.0 and archetype twin 2012 Kentucky (Champion, 0.943 similarity) confirm they are a genuine title contender. Georgia's AdjEM of 19.6 and WAB of 2.6 suggest a solid-but-limited 8-seed. Michigan's Barthag of 0.967 is elite — Georgia has no path to an upset against this quality.

0.8 AdjEM gap with Texas Tech's Barthag of 0.944 edging Alabama's 0.934. TTU's elite AdjD of 93.6 is the differentiator — it will challenge Alabama's offense in a way few teams can. Alabama's higher WAB (6.5 vs 5.0) shows better quality wins, but TTU's defensive profile and archetype twin (2024 Purdue Runner-Up) suggest better tournament suitability. Razor-thin edge to Tech.

0.5 AdjEM gap is functionally zero, but Virginia's WAB of 7.8 vs Tennessee's 4.1 is the decisive tiebreaker — Virginia has proven dramatically more against quality opponents. Barthag is nearly identical (0.943 vs 0.941). Both have strong E8/F4 archetypes. Virginia's quality-of-schedule validation and marginally higher seed give them the slimmest possible edge.

9.9 AdjEM gap is substantial. Iowa State's AdjEM of 28.2 and Barthag of 0.958 rank 6th nationally. Santa Clara played well in R64 but their AdjEM of 21.3 cannot match Iowa State's elite profile. Iowa State's archetype twin 2024 Purdue (Runner-Up, 0.944 similarity) confirms they are built for deep runs. Santa Clara's upset run ends here.

sweet_16.reasoning: 11.6 AdjEM gap with Duke's 37.3 AdjEM being the best in the entire field. Duke's WAB of 13.7 dwarfs St. John's 7.3, confirming elite quality wins. St. John's archetype twin 2023 UConn (Champion) is intriguing but Duke's own archetypes (2024 Purdue Runner-Up, 2025 Florida Champion) are equally deep-run caliber at a higher efficiency tier.

1.4 AdjEM gap is razor-thin but UConn's WAB of 8.8 vs MSU's 7.4 and Barthag of 0.952 vs 0.944 indicate a slightly higher quality tier. Both teams map to 2024 Purdue (Runner-Up) as top archetype twin. UConn's two recent championships (2023, 2024) reflect a program with tournament DNA that the data alone doesn't fully capture. The slimmest of edges.

5.7 AdjEM gap favors Florida with Barthag of 0.973 vs Vanderbilt's 0.946. Florida's archetype twin is 2025 Florida (Champion, 0.953 similarity) — a repeat of last year's champion profile. Vanderbilt's archetypes (2016 Villanova Champion) suggest they could spring an upset, but Florida's WAB of 8.5 and overall efficiency profile are a tier above this SEC rival.

0.8 AdjEM gap technically favors Illinois, but Houston's WAB of 9.0 vs Illinois's 6.6 is the decisive metric — Houston has been dramatically better against quality opponents. Houston's Barthag of 0.971 edges Illinois's 0.968. Houston's AdjD of 89.5 (2nd nationally) will turn this into a defensive slugfest that favors their style. Houston's archetypes (2015 Wisconsin Runner-Up) have deeper tournament pedigree.

9.3 AdjEM gap is substantial at the Sweet 16 level. Arizona's AdjEM of 35.5 and WAB of 13.7 are among the best in the field. Archetype twins 2025 Florida (Champion) and 2012 Kentucky (Champion) signal the highest ceiling in the West. Arkansas's archetype twin 2016 Oklahoma (F4) shows they could compete, but the efficiency gap is too wide.

6.8 AdjEM gap with Purdue's 33.0 AdjEM substantially above Gonzaga's 26.3. Purdue's WAB of 9.2 vs Gonzaga's 7.4 and Barthag of 0.964 vs 0.944 confirm a clear quality tier difference. Gonzaga's archetypes (2025 Tennessee E8, 2018 Michigan Runner-Up) are strong but Purdue's archetype twin 2024 Purdue (Runner-Up) is literally themselves — a known tournament performer.

9.1 AdjEM gap with Michigan's 36.6 AdjEM ranking 2nd in the field. Michigan's WAB of 14.0 is the highest in the entire tournament and their Barthag of 0.980 is near-perfect. Archetype twin 2012 Kentucky (Champion, 0.943 similarity) is the gold standard. Texas Tech's elite defense (AdjD 93.6) will make this competitive but Michigan's offensive firepower is overwhelming.

4.7 AdjEM gap in Iowa State's favor with Barthag of 0.966 vs Virginia's 0.943. Iowa State's archetype twins (2024 Purdue Runner-Up, 2025 Houston Runner-Up) both reached championship games. Virginia's WAB of 7.8 edges ISU's 7.4, but Iowa State's overall efficiency profile and deep-run archetypes carry the day. Iowa State's offensive balance (AdjO 121.5) will stretch Virginia's defensive scheme.

elite_8.reasoning: 9.2 AdjEM gap is substantial at the Elite 8 level. Duke's 37.3 AdjEM is the best in the field and their WAB of 13.7 dwarfs UConn's 8.8. Duke's archetype twins (2025 Florida Champion, 2024 Purdue Runner-Up) project a championship ceiling. UConn's recent titles give them intangible tournament DNA, but the efficiency gap is too large to overcome.

0.9 AdjEM gap is functionally zero, making this the closest Elite 8 matchup. Houston's WAB of 9.0 edges Florida's 8.5 and their AdjD of 89.5 is the best in the country. However, Florida's archetype twin is literally 2025 Florida (Champion, 0.953 similarity) — the same program that won it all last year with a nearly identical statistical profile. Houston's archetypes cap at Runner-Up. In a coin flip, the champion archetype and Florida's AdjO of 125.6 are the tiebreakers.

2.5 AdjEM gap with Arizona's WAB of 13.7 vs Purdue's 9.2 — a massive quality-wins differential. Arizona's archetype twins are 2025 Florida (Champion) and 2012 Kentucky (Champion) — both won it all. Purdue's archetype twin is 2024 Purdue (Runner-Up) — a team that fell short in the final. Arizona's offensive ceiling (AdjO 128.7) gives them the edge in a high-stakes shootout.

5.5 AdjEM gap with Michigan's 36.6 AdjEM ranking 2nd in the field. Michigan's WAB of 14.0 is the highest in the entire tournament — no team has proven more against quality opponents. Archetype twin 2012 Kentucky (Champion, 0.943 similarity) is the ultimate validation. Iowa State's archetypes cap at Runner-Up (2024 Purdue). Michigan's Barthag of 0.980 projects dominance.

final_four.reasoning: Arizona's 1.7 AdjEM edge is marginal but their WAB of 13.7 vs Florida's 8.5 is the decisive factor — Arizona has been dramatically better against quality opponents all season. Both teams map to champion archetypes (Arizona: 2025 Florida, 2012 Kentucky; Florida: 2025 Florida itself). Arizona's AdjO of 128.7 creates an offensive ceiling Florida's defense may struggle to contain.

0.7 AdjEM gap is functionally zero between these two juggernauts. Duke's 37.3 AdjEM is the single best in the field, but Michigan's 36.6 is right behind with a marginally higher WAB (14.0 vs 13.7). Duke's AdjO of 128.1 gives them the slightest offensive edge. Both map to champion archetypes. In the most evenly matched Final Four game imaginable, Duke's top-line efficiency rating is the only differentiator.

championship.reasoning: 1.8 AdjEM gap in Duke's favor — the team with the best AdjEM (37.3) in the entire 2026 field. Identical WAB of 13.7 for both teams. Arizona's archetype twins (2025 Florida Champion, 2012 Kentucky Champion) project a champion at higher similarity scores than Duke's champion twin (2025 Florida at 0.87). However, Duke's AdjO of 128.1 is the best nationally and their 37.3 AdjEM has been the most dominant metric all season. In a razor-thin championship matchup, the team with the highest overall efficiency rating gets the nod.
